title: Eugenia neoformosa
intro: Eugenia neoformosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as a variety of the Brazilian guava. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia neoformosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Eugenia neoformosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
taxonomy: Eugenia neoformosa is classified in the family Myrtaceae. This classification places it within the larger order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants. Eugenia neoformosa is a member of the genus Eugenia, a diverse group of plants commonly known as the "cloves" or "myrtle family". The family Myrtaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Eugenia neoformosa's classification is also part of the angiosperm clade, which includes all flowering plants.

benefits: Eugenia neoformosa is a species of plant that has been studied for its potential health benefits. The leaves of the plant have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Eugenia neoformosa may also have antimicrobial properties, which could help to prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and other microorganisms. preparations: Eugenia neoformosa, also known as the Guanabani or Brazilian guava, has been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes. Herbal preparations made from the plant's leaves and fruits are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. The plant's extracts are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In folk medicine, Eugenia neoformosa is used to treat skin conditions, including wounds and ulcers.
